content.cn_text: 第二部分。你将听到一位公司经理向员工讲解如果发生火灾时应遵循的程序。
content.en_text: Part 2. You will hear a company manager talking to staff about procedures to follow if a fire

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第3页的第11至14题。
content.en_text: breaks out at work. First, you have some time to look at questions 11-14 on page 3.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第11至14题。
content.en_text: Now, listen carefully and answer questions 11-14.

content.cn_text: 大家好，感谢各位准时到场。这次会议会比较短，
content.en_text: Hello, and thanks to everyone for being on time. The meeting will be fairly short and will

content.cn_text: 只讨论消防程序。今天早些时候，我已经和高级消防官开过一次类似会议，
content.en_text: only be looking at fire procedures. I had a similar meeting with the senior fire officers earlier

content.cn_text: 但你们今天在这里，是因为你们自愿担任各自
content.en_text: today, but you're all here because you've volunteered to act as fire wardens for your own

content.cn_text: 部门的消防管理员，而且你们中有些人是第一次担任这个角色。
content.en_text: particular department, and for some of you this will be your first time in the role.

content.cn_text: 虽然你们来到这里后可能已经参加过几次消防演习，
content.en_text: Although you'll probably have taken part in several fire drills since you've been here,

content.cn_text: 但现在施工人员已经完工，我们有了几条新的走廊和楼梯，
content.en_text: but now the construction workers finished and we have a couple of new corridors and stairways,

content.cn_text: 所以我们需要这次会议来更新大家的信息。趁我还没忘，你们可能注意到上周有一些
content.en_text: we need the meeting to update everyone. And before I forget, you might have noticed a bit of

content.cn_text: 升级。手持式灭火器，也就是墙上的标准灭火器，
content.en_text: upgrading last week. The hand held fire extinguishers, the standard ones on the wall,

content.cn_text: 报告显示它们都运作良好，顶部的喷淋系统也是如此，
content.en_text: the report says they're all working well, as is the overhead sprinkler system,

content.cn_text: 但旧的警报系统已经被更换，应该会可靠得多。
content.en_text: but the old alarm system has been replaced and should be a lot more reliable.

content.cn_text: 总之，作为消防管理员，你们有许多职责。
content.en_text: Anyway, as fire wardens, you have a number of responsibilities.

content.cn_text: 好，负责厂房的消防管理员，你们应该记得我们在
content.en_text: Okay, those wardens responsible for the factory buildings, you'll remember we had a minor fire

content.cn_text: 一月份发生过一次小火灾。幸运的是，房间周围的防火门阻止了大部分烟雾外泄，
content.en_text: back in January. Luckily, the fire doors around the room stopped most of the smoke from getting out,

content.cn_text: 但警报本应该更早按下。据我了解，员工有些
content.en_text: but the alarm should have been pressed a lot earlier. From what I understand, staff felt somewhat

content.cn_text: 不愿意自己启动警报，而是在找级别更高的人，所以请确保
content.en_text: reluctant to activate it themselves and were looking for someone senior, so please ensure that

content.cn_text: 每个人都知道立即采取行动是他们自己的责任。你们也能否确保
content.en_text: everyone knows it's their responsibility to take action immediately. Can you also make sure that

content.cn_text: 如果之后有新员工入职，他们已经阅读过消防通知？而负责
content.en_text: if anyone new has been hired since then, that they've read the fire notices? And wardens for

content.cn_text: 办公部门的消防管理员，你们也需要和自己的员工谈一谈。当一月份那次小火灾中
content.en_text: office departments, you'll also have to have a word with your staff. When the fire alarm went off

content.cn_text: 火警响起时，人们很快离开了办公楼。这一点没问题，
content.en_text: in January in that minor fire, people got out of the office building pretty quickly. That was fine,

content.cn_text: 但他们出来之后，就站在门口正前方，所以如果消防队需要
content.en_text: but once they were out, they stood right in front of the doors, so if the fire crews had needed to

content.cn_text: 进入办公室，就会被挡住。值得肯定的是，大家都把包
content.en_text: access the offices, they would have been prevented. Good to see that everyone had left their bags

content.cn_text: 和个人物品留在了里面。不过，在听剩余内容之前，你有一些时间查看
content.en_text: and stuff behind though. Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at

content.cn_text: 第4页的第15至20题。
content.en_text: questions 15 to 20 on page 4.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第15至20题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 15 to 20.

content.cn_text: 好，议程的下一项是消防安全的几个方面，我们需要和
content.en_text: Okay, next on the agenda, there's a few aspects of fire safety that we need to go through with

content.cn_text: 各位消防管理员过一遍。首先，我们要确保下次消防演习时，没有任何东西
content.en_text: all you wardens. Firstly, we want to make sure that when we have the next fire drill, there's nothing

content.cn_text: 挡住逃生路线。我的意思是没有障碍物，所以请尽快
content.en_text: blocking the way in the escape routes. No obstacles, I mean, so please have a look at that as soon as

content.cn_text: 检查这一点。我们希望能够非常迅速地到达室外。另一个绝对应该放在
content.en_text: possible. We want to be able to get outside very quickly. One thing that should definitely be on

content.cn_text: 你们待办事项上的事情，是确保没有人锁上防火门，并且防火门容易通过。
content.en_text: your to-do list is making sure no one's locked the fire doors and that it's easy enough to get through

content.cn_text: 现在，你们可能记得去年有一位消防官来访，向我们展示
content.en_text: them. Now, you might remember that last year we had a fire officer come and visit and show us what

content.cn_text: 如果看到同事开始受到吸入烟雾影响时该怎么做。嗯，同一位
content.en_text: to do if we see a colleague starting to suffer from the effects of smoke inhalation. Well, the same

content.cn_text: 消防官会回来主持另一场培训。另一个问题是如何操作灭火器。
content.en_text: officer is coming back to lead another session. Another issue is how to handle the fire extinguishers.

content.cn_text: 当地消防部门已经安排人来教大家如何操作。我认为
content.en_text: Someone from the local fire department has booked in to teach people how to do that. I think

content.cn_text: 消防管理员和部门员工都参加这些培训会比较好。
content.en_text: it would be a good idea if fire wardens and department staff attended those sessions.

content.cn_text: 看看清单上还有什么。哦，对了，疏散点。主停车场后面的绿地、
content.en_text: Let's see what else is on the list. Oh, yes, the evacuation points. The green area behind the main

content.cn_text: Hunter Street 的人行道，以及工厂停车场后面未使用的区域。
content.en_text: car park, the pavement on Hunter Street, and the unused area behind the factory car park.

content.cn_text: 消防管理员需要确保所有员工都熟悉这些疏散地点，
content.en_text: Fire wardens need to make sure all staff are familiar with these locations for evacuation,

content.cn_text: 所以请在会议结束后立即发送邮件。
content.en_text: so please send our emails directly after the meeting.

content.cn_text: 最后一点与易燃液体有关。我知道之前有人担心
content.en_text: And the last point is to do with flammable liquids. I believe there have been a few concerns

content.cn_text: 它们在仓库里的存放位置，但现在这个问题已经解决了。
content.en_text: about where they were being stored in the warehouse, but that's been resolved now.

content.cn_text: 不过，如果有人还有其他事情要报告，请务必告诉我。
content.en_text: However, if anyone has anything else to report, do let me know.

content.cn_text: 第二部分到此结束。现在你有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section two. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 11–14

items.title: The company is having this meeting about fire procedures because

options.title: A
options.content: employees did badly in the last annual fire drill.

options.title: B
options.content: there have been changes in the building layout.

options.title: C
options.content: new staff have joined the company.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第11题问公司为什么召开这次关于消防程序的会议，原文说有新的 corridors and stairways，需要 update everyone，对应 B “there have been changes in the building layout”。

items.title: There has been a recent upgrade to
items.analysis: 答案解析：第12题问最近升级了什么，原文说 old alarm system has been replaced，对应 C “the alarm system”。

items.title: During the minor fire in January, some staff working in the factory

options.title: A
options.content: were unable to read fire notices.

options.title: C
options.content: were unwilling to start the fire alarm.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第13题问一月份小火灾期间，工厂员工的问题是什么，原文说 reluctant to activate it themselves，对应 C “were unwilling to start the fire alarm”。

items.title: In the fire in January, the problem with office staff was that they

options.title: A
options.content: refused to leave personal items behind.

options.title: B
options.content: moved too slowly during the evacuation.

options.title: C
options.content: did not move far away enough from the building.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第14题问一月份火灾中办公室员工的问题是什么，原文说 stood right in front of the doors，会阻碍消防队进入，对应 C “did not move far away enough from the building”。

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter from the box, A, B or C, next to Questions 15–20.
groups.title: Questions 15–20

list.title: A
list.content: It should be a priority for fire wardens

list.title: B
list.content: It will be dealt with by an external specialist

items.title: ensuring there are no obstacles in fire escape routes
items.analysis: 答案解析：第15题问确保逃生路线无障碍这一项的评价，原文说 please have a look at that as soon as possible，说明是消防管理员的优先事项，对应 A。

items.title: checking that fire doors are easily opened
items.analysis: 答案解析：第16题问检查防火门是否容易打开这一项的评价，原文说 should definitely be on your to-do list，对应 A “It should be a priority for fire wardens”。

items.title: showing staff how to look after each other
items.analysis: 答案解析：第17题问教员工如何照看彼此这一项，原文说 fire officer is coming back to lead another session，由外部消防官处理，对应 B。

items.title: training staff to use fire extinguishers correctly
items.analysis: 答案解析：第18题问培训员工正确使用灭火器这一项，原文说 someone from the local fire department 会来教，由外部专家处理，对应 B。

items.title: checking that staff are aware of evacuation points
items.analysis: 答案解析：第19题问检查员工是否知道疏散点这一项，原文说 fire wardens need to make sure，并要求会后立刻发邮件，对应 A。

items.title: checking that flammable liquids are properly stored
items.analysis: 答案解析：第20题问检查易燃液体是否妥善存放这一项，原文说 that’s been resolved now，说明不需要关注，对应 C “It does not require attention”。
